feedback_addressed: Added 3 SELL recommendations: WMT (46x PE unjustifiable), CL (1.4% growth at 33x PE), HSY (cocoa crisis + GLP-1 double whammy)
Added sector valuation heat map with current PE vs 5-year and 10-year averages for every pick with RED/YELLOW/GREEN signals
Quantified GLP-1 impact per pick: PEP -1.8% revenue drag (HIGH), KO -1.2% (MODERATE), MDLZ -1.5% (MODERATE-HIGH), HSY -2.5% (VERY HIGH)
Fixed PM financial data: negative equity explained as buyback/acquisition artifact, not solvency concern, with FCF coverage of 3.7x
Added entry and exit criteria for each pick with valuation ceilings, fundamental deterioration triggers, and stop-losses
Added scenario analysis: PE compression to 20x (35% probability) vs elevated multiples persist (45%) vs stagflation (20%)
Added COST membership fee elasticity analysis with historical churn data (<1% on increases) and next increase estimate (2029-2030)
Added PM IQOS FDA scenario analysis with probability-weighted outcomes (55% full approval, 25% conditional, 15% delayed, 5% denial)
Added private label competition analysis by category with share data and threat levels
Added input cost sensitivity analysis for cocoa, coffee, corn, tobacco leaf, and packaging
Added Walmart advertising revenue analysis comparing to Amazon's ad economics
Added position sizing: PM 8% (highest), PEP 7%, KO 6%, MDLZ 5%, COST 4%, GIS 4%
Corrected COST scoring math noted in v1 by using verified formula calculations
feedback_ignored: KO $18B IRS tax dispute still not quantified in per-share impact terms despite v1 specifically demanding this
remaining_flaws: GIS added as a contrarian pick at deep value (10.4x PE) but the analysis is thin -- why is it so cheap, and what specifically catalyzes a re-rating?
COST at 4% allocation still seems high given the DEEP RED valuation heat map signal at 53x PE, even though the membership model is acknowledged as high-quality

PEP SELL thesis from v1 (3% revenue growth, value trap) is partially addressed by highlighting the 20% discount to 5-year average PE, but the analyst pivoted from skepticism to BUY without fully resolving the slow-growth concern
what_impressed: WMT SELL at 46x PE (103% above 10-year average) is a bold and well-reasoned call that directly addresses the v1 flaw of buying everything in an overvalued sector
GLP-1 impact analysis with category-level volume data (savory snacks -10%, sugary beverages -7%, yogurt +5%) is research that most sell-side does not provide
PM IQOS FDA probability analysis with 4 scenarios and EPS impact for each demonstrates proper binary event analysis
Valuation heat map immediately identifies where the risk is (WMT DEEP RED, COST RED) vs where value exists (PEP GREEN, GIS DEEP GREEN)
Private label competition analysis by category with 24% unit share and rising shows the analyst understands structural headwinds
Input cost sensitivity table covering 5 commodity categories with projected 2026 changes and per-pick exposure is the kind of detail that was completely absent in v1
